title: carlina acanthifolia subsp. acanthifolia
intro: Carlina acanthifolia subsp. acanthifolia, also known as the carline thistle, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Carlina acanthifolia subsp. acanthifolia can be found in infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often used to treat a range of conditions, including skin irritations and rheumatism.
